name: IDIOPATHIC PULMONARY FIBROSIS
definition: USUAL INTERSTITIAL PNEUMONIA specific form of chronic progressive fibrosing interstitial pneumonia of unknown cause clinical syndrome associated with morphologic pattern of UIP and related to cigarette

demographics.Histo: UIP pattern = cluster of fibroblasts + immature connective tissue within pulmonary

discriminating_features: decreased lung volume, progressive over time

CXR: decreased lung volume, progressive over time
lower zone macrocystic honeycombing (90%) often combined with traction
irregular reticular opacities (= intralobular interstitial thickening) in peripheral basal
ground-glass opacities in area of fibrosis (occasionally)
coexisting emphysema (common) makes Dx less reliable

clinical.Rx: cyclosporine A + corticosteroids (for acute exacerbations only); lung transplantation
clinical.Prognosis: poor with invariable deterioration and a mean survival of 2.5-3.5 years (30-50%
